name: Traditional Decorated Christmas Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Lemon peel; finely grated (finely grated)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------ICING-----------------------------------
1. In a large bowl, beat together egg whites and water until foamy.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar, continuing to beat until thick and creamy.
3. Tint with food coloring, if desired.
4. Spread icing evenly over cooled cookies.
5. Decorate with glitter, sprinkles, confetti, and cinnamon red hots.
1. Beat butter and sugar in a small bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Add egg, lemon peel, vanilla, and salt; beat well.
3. Gradually add flour; beat until thoroughly incorporated.
4. Add water, a few drops at a time, only until the dough starts to come away from the side of the bowl.
5. Wrap dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate until firm (about 2 hours).
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Lightly grease four cookie sheets with butter or cooking spray.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out dough to 1/8 inch thickness.
9. Cut into various shapes using cookie cutters or homemade cardboard patterns, or freehand.
10. Roll scraps and cut again if needed.
11. Make small holes in the cookies if you plan to hang them (using a wooden pick).
12. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until beginning to brown around the edges.
13. Remove cookies from oven and transfer to wire racks to cool completely before decorating.
